title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
summary: Jacob E. Thomas argues that existing persona-evaluation benchmarks fail to detect "Miranda distortion," where cultural representations in training data overwhelm primary documentary records, causing AI systems to produce culturally composite personas rather than record-grounded ones. He proposes a shift from cognitive to epistemic simulation evals using domain experts and pre-registered protocols like the Prism Experiment to catch anachronistic reasoning that automated metrics miss.
writeup: Jacob E. Thomas argues that current persona-evaluation benchmarks miss what he calls Miranda distortion: cases where cultural representations in training data overwhelm primary documentary records, leading models to generate culturally composite personas instead of record-grounded ones. The relevance is that automated metrics, including LLM-as-a-judge approaches, fail to catch this distortion and the anachronistic reasoning it produces. The proposed fix is a move from cognitive to epistemic simulation evals, drawing on domain experts and pre-registered protocols such as the Prism Experiment.
